opp.name: Beko_P2P
opp.next_step: June 19: Caroline confirmed that proposals are still valid in their current form to BEKO - next invitation to demos in July 04/05/2026: Email from Yildrey: We would like to present for your attention the updated project timeline shared below, and express our desire to meet with you soon to hear your suggestions for solutions. Before going futher, we kindly ask you to confirm your participation in the project once again. We kindly request that you provide your confirmation by the end of business on Friday, May 8, which is the first step in the timeline. Description/Step Last Update Participation Declaration 8.05.2026 Bidder’s Questions 15.05.2026 Customer’s Answers 22.05.2026 RFP Response Date (Commercial and Technical Proposal) 29.05.2026 Customer’s Evaluation of Responses and Short-listing Demo session if it is required 31.07.2026 Short List Announcement 28.08.2026 Final Offers by Short-listed Bidders 18.09.2026 Announcement of Winner 9.10.2026 ﻿
opp.ais_why:
• The SF Description focuses entirely on traditional priorities: 'Beko is looking to replace their current SAP-based procurement system before end-of-life in 2027... Initial scope is procure-to-pay for indirect and investment procurement... requires integration with multiple ERP systems, HR for approvals, and custom in-house applications... e-commerce-like catalog experience and flexible approval workflows... Turkish-speaking consultants are desired... currently in the market research phase, gathering information before issuing an RFP.' Zero AI/GenAI/agentic language anywhere.
• Formal Evaluation but BD_Engagement is Unengaged; Beko is in pre-RFP market-research phase with phased rollout across 9 countries (potentially expanding to 30+ subsidiaries after the Whirlpool partnership). May 2026 meeting attended by Pranshu Sahay and Hrishikesh from Zycus side. Last activity 5 May 2026. Product scope (eProcurement, iRequest, Merlin) bundles Merlin but the entire Description focus is multi-country phased SAP-displacement, not AI transformation.
• Medium confidence: SF Description is rich (869 chars) and explicit on traditional procurement priorities with no AI dimension. The Avoma summary for this opp returned data from an unrelated LCWaikiki account (fuzzy-match error), so we have no direct Avoma-transcript AI signal. Default DOWN at 3/4 boundary applied; this is a textbook traditional P2P replacement with AI bundled as Zycus add-on.

sources: SF Description (SAP EOL 2027 replacement, 9-country phased rollout)
SF Products__c: eProcurement;iRequest;Merlin
SF Next Step: RFP commercial proposal 29 May 2026
Avoma: zero matched buyer calls
metrics.narrative: No quantified business case is on record: Customer_Business_Problem__c / Business_Objectives__c / Value_to_Customer__c carry no numbers, there are zero buyer calls to mine, and the RFP commercial proposal (submitted 29 May 2026 per Next Step) was priced without a documented savings model. The only articulated driver is qualitative and structural: Beko must replace its SAP-based procurement system before end-of-life in 2027 (SF Description), with a phased rollout across 9 countries potentially expanding to 30+ subsidiaries after the Whirlpool partnership. Without a cost/cycle-time/headcount savings case validated with the buyer, Zycus pricing will be exposed at the 18 Sep final-offers gate against any rival that quantifies value better. Quantifying ROI and validating it with Beko finance is a ranked move.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: none flagged Champion
SF tasks 2026-06-11/17: procedural RFP correspondence with Yildiray Seker
Avoma: zero matched buyer calls
champion.narrative: No champion or internal coach exists. None of the 10 Contact Roles is flagged Champion, and there is zero evidence of advocacy, internal selling, or competitive intel-sharing in tasks, Next Step, or calls. The most active buyer contact is Yildiray Seker, but the correspondence (11-17 Jun 2026) is procedural RFP coordination, not advocacy for Zycus. In a competitive, procurement-led RFP with a 7-week evaluation blackout (29 May to 31 Jul), the absence of a champion leaves Zycus unable to see scoring, competitor positioning, or how to tailor the 31 Jul demo. Developing a champion, most plausibly Hanife Eda Budak (business pain owner) or Osman Karslioglu (senior IT), is the highest-leverage gap and a ranked move.

sources: SF Description (SAP EOL 2027 replacement)
SF OpportunityContactRole: Tulay Toker (SAP specialist, Evaluator)
SF Competitors__c: blank
Living memory (prior sweep inferred SAP Ariba)
competition.narrative: The competitive field is unconfirmed because Competitors__c is blank and Zycus has had zero buyer calls, but the shape is inferable. The incumbent is Beko's own SAP-based procurement system, being displaced ahead of its 2027 end-of-life (SF Description), so this is a rip-and-replace with a real compelling event. SAP Ariba is the inferred live peer threat: Beko is a SAP shop and a SAP specialist (Tulay Toker) sits on the evaluation team, giving any SAP-ecosystem successor a native-integration and internal-ally advantage. No other rival is named in any source. How Zycus wins: lead with a purpose-built source-to-pay suite plus Turkish-speaking consultants and Merlin AI as a Phase-2 differentiator against SAP's ERP-centric complexity, and confirm the actual RFP shortlist in the July demo.

sources: SF Description (SAP EOL 2027, indirect/investment P2P, 9-country rollout)
SF OpportunityContactRole: Hanife Eda Budak (Head - Indirect Purchasing)
identify_pain.narrative: The core pain is confirmed and structural: Beko's SAP-based procurement system is reaching end-of-life in 2027, forcing replacement, with initial scope procure-to-pay for indirect and investment procurement and a phased rollout across 9 countries (potentially 30+ subsidiaries after the Whirlpool partnership) (SF Description). Secondary needs are an e-commerce-like catalog experience, flexible approval workflows, and multi-ERP/HR/custom-app integration, implying frustration with the current system's usability and reach. The pain is real but articulated at the requirements level, not quantified, and the pain owner is inferred (Hanife Eda Budak, Head - Indirect Purchasing, for the business side; the IT team for the platform side) rather than voiced on a call. Getting the pain owner on a live call to quantify impact is the gap to close.

sources: SF Description (SAP EOL 2027 forcing function)
SF Next Step: winner announced 9 Oct vs 30 Nov close
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are unknown: no MSA/SOW, legal, procurement-channel, or InfoSec detail is captured in any field or activity. The forcing function is clear though: the current SAP-based procurement system reaches end-of-life in 2027 (SF Description), and the buyer's own timeline leaves just 51 days between the 9 Oct winner announcement and the 30 Nov close for legal, InfoSec, and multi-ERP integration scoping. For a multi-country manufacturer this is tight and likely to slip. Visibility would come from asking the buyer, once shortlisted, for their contracting and security-review steps and the SAP-integration validation plan.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Dilek Beyhan (Decision Maker), Osman Karslioglu (IT Corporate Solutions Director)
SF budget-approval fields blank
Avoma: zero matched buyer calls
economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is unmapped. The only Contact Role flagged Decision Maker is Dilek Beyhan (Head - IT Logistics & Purchasing Solutions), an operational IT leader, not the budget authority for a $267k, multi-country, board-visible SAP-displacement program. The senior-most mapped contact is Osman Karslioglu (IT Corporate Solutions Director), a plausible executive sponsor but with no evidence of engagement in tasks, calls, or Next Step. No budget-approval field (Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c) is populated and no C-level finance/procurement sponsor appears anywhere. Mapping the budget owner (likely a CFO or Group CPO for a program of this scale and multi-country reach) is a ranked move before the 28 Aug shortlist.

sources: SF Next Step: full RFP milestone timeline
SF task 2026-06-17: shortlisting-timeline correspondence with Yildiray Seker
decision_process.narrative: The buyer's decision process is unusually well-defined by their own RFP schedule (Next Step): participation declaration 8 May, bidder questions 15 May, customer answers 22 May, RFP response 29 May, demo session 31 Jul, shortlist announcement 28 Aug, final offers 18 Sep, winner announced 9 Oct. This is a procurement-led, milestone-gated evaluation with a formal short-listing stage, and Zycus has committed to participate (Caroline, 19 Jun). What is missing is the internal approval chain behind the announcement (who signs off the award, whether board or group-level approval is required for the multi-country rollout) since Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c and Multiple_approvals__c are not populated. Reading the process this way, the near-term gate is the 31 Jul demo, which decides shortlist entry on 28 Aug.

sources: SF Description (P2P scope, ERP/HR integration, catalog UX, Turkish consultants)
SF Next Step: RFP timeline with bidder Q&A and demo session
decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are inferable from the SF Description and RFP structure but not confirmed on a call: procure-to-pay for indirect and investment procurement, integration with multiple ERP systems plus HR for approvals and custom in-house apps, an e-commerce-like catalog experience, flexible approval workflows, and Turkish-speaking consultants. The buyer is running a formal, weighted RFP (bidder questions 15 May, answers 22 May, technical and commercial response 29 May, demo 31 Jul), so criteria are documented in the RFP itself. What is unknown is the scoring weights and Zycus's relative standing, because there is no champion feeding back and no buyer call. Confirming the demo evaluation rubric with the buyer in July is the way to close this.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — Field is unconfirmed (Competitors__c blank, zero buyer calls). The incumbent being displaced is Beko's own SAP-based procurement system (EOL 2027, the compelling event). The single strongest inferred current threat is SAP Ariba as the SAP-ecosystem successor, given a SAP specialist (Tulay Toker) on the evaluation team and Beko's SAP footprint; no other rival is named in any source, so the real risk is Zycus's blindness to the actual RFP shortlist rather than a confirmed competitor.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: Beko Global — Formal Evaluation · $267K · closes 30 Nov

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'At Risk' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 21 is carried by momentum 55 vs stage-expected 52. Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 55 (moving forward): Next Step carries 4 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 4 is anchored on the stage win (21), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 55, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 4 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

No Avoma signal exists to over/under-rate this (opp lookup returned an unrelated account).

confidence_signals.summary: Buyer is responsive on the RFP thread (reply 17 Jun, proposals confirmed valid 19 Jun) so the deal is not cooling, but all engagement is email/procedural with no live calls, keeping true confidence low.

north_star_verdict.math: 150 days to close (2026-11-30). Deal age 626 days (created 2024-10-14). In Formal Evaluation with no forward stage progression; close date pushed 30 Sep to 30 Nov on 26 May 2026. Buyer RFP clock: RFP response 29 May, demo session 31 Jul, shortlist 28 Aug, final offers 18 Sep, winner announced 9 Oct, leaving only 51 days (9 Oct to 30 Nov) for MSA/SOW/legal/InfoSec/SAP integration. Pace required: win a shortlist slot by 28 Aug and the award by 9 Oct to preserve a tight contracting window.
evidence: Buyer-controlled RFP timeline (Next Step): RFP response 29 May 2026, demo session 31 Jul, shortlist 28 Aug, final offers 18 Sep, winner 9 Oct
Rep/buyer touch 19 Jun 2026 (Next Step): Caroline confirmed proposals valid; buyer expected to invite Zycus to demos in July
Last Salesforce activity 2026-06-17: buyer contact Yildiray Seker corresponding with Caroline on shortlisting timeline
No champion or coach across Contact Roles, tasks, or calls; Zycus is blind to buyer scoring during the 7-week evaluation blackout
Economic buyer unmapped: the only Decision Maker contact (Dilek Beyhan) is an operational IT lead, not budget authority for a $267k multi-country SaaS rollout
Zero buyer calls ever recorded (Avoma opp lookup returned an unrelated LCWaikiki account); every read is from email only
Verdict trajectory: holding STEADY at At Risk vs prior sweep; the single biggest blocker to close remains no champion access during the competitive evaluation
north_star_verdict.headline: Live buyer-run RFP on schedule (proposals confirmed valid 19 Jun, July demo expected), but Zycus has zero live buyer calls, no champion, and an unmapped economic buyer heading into the 29 May to 31 Jul evaluation blackout.

flags: Develop a champion (Hanife Eda Budak or Osman Karslioglu) before the 28 Aug shortlist to gain scoring visibility during the 29 May-31 Jul evaluation blackout.
Map the economic buyer: Decision Maker Dilek Beyhan is operational IT, so identify the Group CPO who approves a $267k multi-country rollout.
Convert email-only engagement into a live working session to validate SAP-integration scope with the relevant stakeholder and Tulay Toker and de-risk the biggest technical objection.
best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers dominate this deal: no champion during the RFP blackout, an unmapped economic buyer, and a total absence of live buyer calls.
